reasoning: The informal statement asserts that an uncountable subset E of ℝ has a condensation point — a point p such that every neighborhood of p contains uncountably many points of E. The Lean signature encodes this exactly: the hypothesis `¬ Set.Countable E` matches "E is uncountable", and the conclusion `∃ p : ℝ, ∀ U ∈ 𝓝 p, ¬ Set.Countable (U ∩ E)` is precisely the standard definition of a condensation point (every neighborhood intersects E in an uncountable set). The quantifier structure, ambient type (ℝ), and mathematical content all match. No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the conclusion neither strengthens nor weakens the claim.
